title: Prunus × insueta
intro: Prunus × insueta, also known as the apricot kernel tree, is a hybrid species of apricot tree that is primarily cultivated for its edible seeds and kernel. The kernel of Prunus × insueta has been valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to support immune function and act as an antioxidant. The bioactive constituents of Prunus × insueta kernel include amygdalin, a cyanogenic glycoside, as well as other compounds such as vitamin B17 and limonene. Prunus × insueta is commonly used in herbal preparations, including kernel extracts, oils, and supplements, which are often marketed as natural remedies for various health conditions.
taxonomy: Prunus × insueta is classified as a hybrid species within the Prunus genus. This specific classification makes it a type of stone fruit, closely related to other cherry and plum varieties. It shares characteristics with other Prunus species, such as the presence of showy flowers and edible fruit. As a hybrid, Prunus × insueta likely exhibits a unique combination of traits from its parent species.
